title: Bank and post office clerks
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate transportation, storage, or distribution activities in accordance with organizational policies and applicable government laws or regulations. Includes logistics managers. Direct or coordinate production, purchasing, warehousing, distribution, or financial forecasting services or activities to limit costs and improve accuracy, customer service, or safety. Examine existing procedures or opportunities for streamlining activities to meet product distribution needs. Direct the movement, storage, or processing of inventory. Advise and educate individuals or organizations on acquiring and managing debt. May provide guidance in determining the best type of loan and explain loan requirements or restrictions. May help develop debt management plans or student financial aid packages. May advise on credit issues, or provide budget, mortgage, bankruptcy, or student financial aid counseling. (Based on 12 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Balance currency, coin, and checks in cash drawers at ends of shifts and calculate daily transactions, using computers, calculators, or adding machines.
Order a supply of cash to meet daily needs.
Answer telephones and assist customers with their questions.
Quote unit exchange rates, inform customers about foreign currency regulations, and compute transaction fees for currency exchanges.
Issue checks to bond owners and compose, type, and mail customer statements and related correspondence.
Cash checks after verifying signatures, amounts, and account funds.
Compute financial fees, interest, and service charges as needed.
Carry out special services for customers, such as ordering bank cards and checks, and count, verify, and post armored car deposits.
Process and maintain records of customer loans and count daily cash inventories.
Sort and file deposit slips and checks, and monitor bank vaults to ensure cash balances are correct.
Prepare and verify cashier's checks, and receive checks and cash for deposit while verifying amounts and deposit slip accuracy.
Examine checks for endorsements and verify necessary information such as dates and the legality of documents.
Count currency, coins, and checks received to prepare them for deposit or shipment.
Identify transaction mistakes when debits and credits do not balance, and enter customers' transactions into computers to record activities and issue receipts.
Arrange monies received in cash boxes and dispensers by denomination, and process transactions such as deposits and withdrawals.
Receive mortgage, loan, or utility bill payments, verifying payment dates and amounts due while resolving problems or discrepancies with customers' accounts.
Explain, promote, or sell bank products and services tailored to customer needs, such as savings bonds and money orders.
Cash money orders and manage customer inquiries related to financial matters in person or via various communication platforms.
Teach courses or seminars on budgeting and personal financial management.
Advise clients on financial problems, including assisting with budgeting, debt management plans, and housing matters.
Recommend strategies for clients to meet financial goals and refer them to appropriate community resources as needed.
Prepare written documents to communicate financial recommendations to clients and negotiate with creditors on their behalf.
Approve loans within specified limits and review loan applications and agreements for accuracy according to policy.
Maintain and update records of client account activity, ensuring all transactions and counseling notes are documented.
Stay informed about new loan types and financial services to better assist customers and increase sales of banking products.
Meet with applicants to gather information for loan applications and provide insight into the loan process and options.

File sales slips in customers' ledgers for billing purposes, receive charge slips or credit applications by mail, or receive information from salespeople or merchants by telephone, and mail charge statements to customers.

Examine city directories and public records to verify residence property ownership, bankruptcies, liens, arrest records, or unpaid taxes of applicants, and relay credit report information to subscribers by mail or by telephone.
Call customers to collect payment on delinquent accounts and consult with customers to resolve complaints or verify financial or credit transactions.

Prepare reports of findings and recommendations, review individual or commercial customer files to identify and select delinquent accounts for collection, and prepare reports summarizing daily transactions and earnings for individual customer accounts.

Transmit claims for payment or further investigation, prepare insurance claim forms or related documents, check for completeness, calculate amount of claim, and post or attach information to claim file.

Plan, organize, or manage the work of subordinate staff to ensure that the work is accomplished in a manner consistent with organizational requirements, and analyze all aspects of corporate logistics to determine the most cost-effective means of transporting products or supplies.

Analyze information about supplier performance or procurement success, meet with suppliers to discuss performance metrics, and document physical supply chain processes, such as workflows or system flows.

Develop or implement procedures to evaluate or select suppliers, design or implement plant warehousing strategies for production materials, and confer with supply chain planners to forecast demand or create supply plans.

Define performance metrics for measurement or evaluation of supply chain factors, analyze inventories to determine how to increase inventory turns, and participate in the coordination of engineering changes or new product launches.
Collaborate with other departments to integrate logistics with business systems or processes, and design or implement supply chains that support business strategies.

Calculate, review, and correct errors on interest, principal, payment, and closing costs, prepare forms based on transaction requests, and ensure that appropriate equipment and staffing levels are determined to load, unload, move, or store materials.

Negotiate prices and terms with suppliers, select transportation routes to maximize economy by combining shipments, and monitor suppliers' activities to assess performance in meeting quality or delivery requirements.
Review or update supply chain practices in accordance with new or changing environmental policies and regulations.
Monitor daily stock prices and compute fluctuations to determine the need for additional collateral to secure loans, and accept payment on accounts.

Submit loan applications with recommendation for underwriting approval, file and maintain loan records, and contact credit bureaus, employers, and other sources to check applicants' credit and personal references.
Verify and examine information and accuracy of loan applications and closing documents, and interview loan applicants to obtain personal and financial data and assist in completing applications.
Answer questions and advise customers regarding loans and transactions, and present loan and repayment schedules to customers.

Assemble and compile documents for loan closings, such as title abstracts, insurance forms, loan forms, and tax receipts, and monitor customer accounts to determine whether payments are made on time and that other loan terms are being followed.
Execute wire transfers of funds, perform teller duties as required, and perform clerical tasks, such as answering phones or distributing mail.
Collect and record customer deposits and fees and issue receipts, and inform customers of procedures for applying for services, such as ATM cards and direct deposit of checks.

Interview customers to obtain information needed for opening accounts or renting safe-deposit boxes, and compile information about new accounts, enter account information into computers, and file related forms or documents.
Correspond with customers and confer with coworkers to answer inquiries, discuss market fluctuations, or resolve account problems.
Order property and mortgage insurance policies to ensure protection against loss on mortgaged property.
Perform foreign currency transactions and sell traveler's checks, and process loan applications.
Schedule repairs for locks on safe-deposit boxes and schedule and conduct closings of mortgage transactions.
Answer customers' questions and explain available services, such as deposit accounts, bonds, and securities.

Evaluate and select technology solutions to improve tracking and reporting of materials or product distribution, and implement new or improved supply chain processes to improve efficiency or performance.

Supervise the activities of workers engaged in receiving, storing, testing, and shipping products or materials, and design, implement, or oversee product take-back programs to ensure products are recycled or responsibly disposed.

Inspect physical conditions of warehouses, vehicle fleets, or equipment and order testing, maintenance, repairs, or replacements, and design or implement supply chains that support environmental policies.
Investigate and correct errors upon customers' request, according to customer and bank records.
Monitor forecasts and quotas to identify changes and predict effects on supply chain activities, and identify opportunities to reuse or recycle materials to minimize waste.
Locate or select environmentally friendly materials for manufacturing processes, and identify or qualify new suppliers in collaboration with other departments.
Conduct or oversee life cycle analyses of products or systems to determine their environmental impacts.
Duplicate records for branch office distribution and organize detailed office records, maintaining files for policyholders, including policies to be reinstated or cancelled.
